_note: Kyverno controller does NOT enforce on CRC (no admission webhook), so the two admission-REJECTION checks (verify-dev-route-blocked, verify-prod-workload-blocked) cannot reproduce here: the Route/Deployment get admitted instead of denied, so the check's normal verdict is FAIL and we invert it with expect_fail (it only truly passes on a real DCS with Kyverno enforcing). The object-present / positive checks all run for real on CRC. The ClusterPolicy applies as a plain object create (CRDs present, controller absent), so verify-kyverno-policy-present and verify-clusterpolicy-rules read the object as authored.

steps.run: envsubst < hello-dcs.yaml | oc apply -n dev -f - && oc rollout status deployment/hello-dcs -n dev --timeout=120s
envsubst < hello-dcs.yaml | oc apply -n prod -f - && oc rollout status deployment/hello-dcs -n prod --timeout=120s
oc get route hello-dcs -n prod -o jsonpath='{.spec.host}{"\n"}'
